entities.label: the five moves
entities.gloss: PDD's workflow: MAP recovers what the system actually does, PIN locks it in tests, CARVE splits work review-sized, SHIP changes it under gates, PROVE shows behavior held and the debt is gone.

entities.label: operator
entities.gloss: The human owner whose attention is the bottleneck: sizes the work, sets the gates, owns the sign-offs. The agent does the work under those gates.

entities.label: Constitution
entities.gloss: The gate set a project commits to and enforces — its binding law, scaffolded as `.pdd/constitution.md`. Architectural commitments recorded alongside it are prose until a gate enforces them.

entities.label: gate (block / warn / off)
entities.gloss: A deterministic check with a real exit code, run in one of three committed modes: block (a red verdict fails the run), warn (surfaced, non-blocking), or off. Most start warn and earn block.

entities.label: language pack
entities.gloss: A per-language gate implementation behind a JSON contract (TypeScript via ts-morph, Python via libcst), as opposed to the language-agnostic core text gates. Adding a language means implementing the contract.

entities.label: flow
entities.gloss: One per-language section of `.pdd/config.yaml` v2, with its own gates, source scope, test command, and mutation settings. One flow per language; execution currently runs only the first flow.

entities.label: footgun
entities.gloss: A verified name-lie: a name, type, or signature that contradicts real runtime behavior, recorded with the misleading appearance, the verified reality, a file:line anchor, and a last-verified date.

entities.label: system-truth atlas
entities.gloss: The committed deliverable of MAP, in four parts: verified code map, footgun register, ranked risk register, silent-failure census. The recovered spec everything downstream reads from.

entities.label: silent failure
entities.gloss: An error that leaves no trace — a swallowed exception, ignored failure return, unwatched task, unchecked subprocess exit — so a broken system still looks healthy. MAP censuses each site as real-risk or benign.

entities.label: sweep
entities.gloss: MAP's mechanical first stage: `check` (instant), then `prove` (seconds), then `grade` (only with `--grade`). Best-effort: a failed stage is recorded, not fatal, but the resulting problem list may be incomplete.

entities.label: carve
entities.gloss: PDD's CARVE move — split work into review-sized units, riskiest assumption proven first — and the MAP stage that carves findings into the risk-ranked `.pdd/units.json`, deterministic and byte-identical across hosts.

entities.label: unit
entities.gloss: One review-sized piece of the remediation roadmap: a carved group of findings carrying risk and size with an explanatory basis, its fingerprint set, and an id equal to its grade slug.

entities.label: attention-sized
entities.gloss: Work sized so planning takes about one minute of operator attention and reviewing completion about another; split on concerns, not lines of code or file count.

entities.label: finding fingerprint
entities.gloss: A finding's stable identity: `<file>:<offset>:<kind>`, byte-precise so it survives line shifts; findings without a code offset carry a symbolic or line+hash fallback key.

entities.label: riskScore
entities.gloss: A unit's ranking: exposure × (1 + 0.5·normFanIn + 0.5·covGap), where exposure sums kind-weighted findings and silent failures weigh high. Used when every unit has one; otherwise ordering falls back to risk tier.

entities.label: gate budget
entities.gloss: The third sizing axis after unit-attention and PR-attention: a cap on a chunk's mutation cost in machine-minutes, estimated from a calibrated model and re-fit from actual runs; oversized groups split until they fit.

entities.label: roadmap
entities.gloss: MAP's output: the risk-ranked list of units the operator triages at the curate gate and remediation works through — derived by replaying the ledger over the pristine `.pdd/units.json` baseline.

entities.label: curate gate
entities.gloss: The human-in-the-loop pass (`pdd roadmap`) where the operator triages every carved unit — approve, deny, edit, move, split, refactor — over the append-only ledger before sign-off.

entities.label: ledger
entities.gloss: The append-only record of every roadmap decision (`.pdd/roadmap-ledger.json`). The effective roadmap is derived by replaying it over the pristine carve baseline, so re-carving never clobbers a decision; history is never rewritten.

entities.label: sign-off
entities.gloss: The ledger operation that freezes the curated roadmap and hands it to remediation. Requires every unit triaged (zero pending; denied counts too); any later operation reopens the roadmap.

entities.label: disposition
entities.gloss: The curate-gate decision each unit gets. Shipped today: approve, deny, or be consumed by a refactor; the fuller axis (repair in place, retire, accept) is designed, not yet built.

entities.label: refactor (object)
entities.gloss: A first-class ledger object that consumes one or more units and produces one mechanism under a decision-total keep/drop contract; consolidation, plain replace, and greenfield differ only in the consumed count.

entities.label: decision-total
entities.gloss: A contract property: every behavior of the consumed mechanisms is explicitly kept or dropped, and every affected call site migrated or shimmed; anything unassigned blocks the change.

entities.label: blast radius
entities.gloss: A refactor's affected set: the call sites whose behavior must be preserved while their code changes, computed as a dependency-graph query. A lower bound — runtime resolution hides edges — and it picks atomic versus parallel cutover.

entities.label: seam
entities.gloss: An interface boundary you can substitute behind: where dependencies (clock, RNG, I/O) are injected, behavior is pinned, and a replacement mechanism is introduced. Where no seam exists, extract one first.

entities.label: characterization test
entities.gloss: A test capturing code's current behavior, written before any change, so a regression turns it red; the recovered spec. Held to the same adequacy bar as any other test.

entities.label: reproducibility baseline
entities.gloss: A pre-change capture that can prove a change altered nothing: replay-and-diff when deterministic, else golden-master or injected seams for clock, RNG, and I/O. Making the system reproducible is itself a deliverable.

entities.label: pin
entities.gloss: To lock current behavior in a test that fails if it changes. Pinning values means asserting the exact returned value, not a type or truthiness, which a constant could satisfy.

entities.label: boundary test
entities.gloss: For any comparison against a limit, a test with the value at the limit itself, not only either side; the equality case is what pins `<` versus `<=`.

entities.label: no-op path
entities.gloss: The branch where a conditional mutation's trigger is not met and post-state must equal pre-state. Every cleanup, eviction, expiry, or pruning method needs a test for it; re-implementors forget the guard.

entities.label: cover-the-mirror
entities.gloss: The rule that an operation with a natural inverse implies the inverse exists (create/delete, acquire/release) or is marked absent-by-design; also the gate flagging a forward operation whose inverse is missing.

entities.label: property
entities.gloss: An invariant asserted over generated inputs covering the input's shape, not hand-picked examples — sourced from the data's schema or the design, never from reading the implementation.

entities.label: mutant
entities.gloss: A small bug deliberately injected into the code — a flipped comparison, a deleted branch, a constant return — to check whether any test fails.

entities.label: surviving mutant
entities.gloss: An injected bug no test catches. Each survivor is either a weak test (add a case or property) or dead code (delete it); read it before trusting the count.

entities.label: mutation grading
entities.gloss: Grading whether a suite actually bites by injecting many small bugs and checking the tests catch them; in the CLI, `pdd grade` — a broad diagnostic that never fails the build, cached per unit.

entities.label: false-covered boundary
entities.gloss: A threshold constant that tests reference but never pin: `pdd prove` flips its comparison operator, runs those tests, and nothing fails — the equality limit was never actually checked.

entities.label: walking skeleton
entities.gloss: A thin end-to-end spike exercising the real thing — real call, real data, real concurrency, not a mock — run to prove a plan's riskiest assumption before anything is built on it.

entities.label: derisk gate
entities.gloss: A CARVE gate: before decomposing work, prove the riskiest architectural assumptions with a walking skeleton and emit a ranked risk register and a GREEN/RED/OVERRIDE verdict; skips must be explicit, never silent.

entities.label: decision gate
entities.gloss: A gate on a decision you can't cheaply walk back: name the observable kill-condition that would prove it wrong, wire a detector, and size the proof to the cost of being wrong.

entities.label: kill-condition
entities.gloss: The observable condition that would prove a decision wrong, named before anything is built on the decision, with a detector watching for it. A decision without one reads the same right or wrong.

entities.label: classify
entities.gloss: The MAP step that triages findings by verdict. Only high/medium-severity findings ever spend tokens, and a dropping verdict (`equivalent`/`benign`) requires a rationale before the validated merge accepts it.

entities.label: Model B / write boundary
entities.gloss: The agent contract: the session authors content (atlas fragments, verdicts, fixes); the CLI alone validates and writes. Nothing model-authored becomes `.pdd/` state unvalidated, save two audit/cache artifacts.

entities.label: remediation
entities.gloss: The post-sign-off loop that fixes approved units in isolated worktrees under the same gates, one commit per unit on the `pdd/remediate` branch. `committed` means awaiting your merge, not resolved.

entities.label: worktree sandbox
entities.gloss: A detached git worktree where mutation runs or remediation fixes execute in isolation, so the operator's working tree is never touched; a sandbox that fails its baseline re-runs sequentially in place.

entities.label: needs-hitl
entities.gloss: A remediation unit parked for the human: the fix touched files outside the unit's scope, the post-fix suite regressed, or its findings aren't check-class so the re-sweep gate can't certify them.

entities.label: re-sweep gate
entities.gloss: The remediation certification step: `pdd check` re-run inside the worktree against post-fix code. Today it certifies check-class findings only; prove/grade/atlas-class findings park at needs-hitl by design.

entities.label: re-map to reconcile
entities.gloss: How resolution is detected after you merge remediation commits: re-run `pdd map` — the finding clears, the re-carve drops the unit, and its status row is pruned. Never by polling git.

entities.label: Foundation
entities.summary: Implementation is cheap; expert attention is the bottleneck. The acceptance set is the spec, not just its validation.

entities.label: MAP
entities.summary: Recover ground truth — read the system; the names lie.

entities.label: PIN
entities.summary: Lock current behavior before changing it.

entities.label: CARVE
entities.summary: Cut work into attention-sized, de-risked units.

entities.label: SHIP
entities.summary: Make each change under mechanical gates.

entities.label: PROVE
entities.summary: Show behavior preserved and debt burned down.

entities.label: Project rules
entities.summary: Rules the project commits to and enforces.

entities.label: Trustworthy tests
entities.summary: A passing suite only means something if the tests would fail when the behavior is wrong and no error can hide.

entities.label: Behavior truth & preservation
entities.summary: Recover what the code actually does, then prove a change didn't alter it.

entities.label: Tool discipline
entities.summary: Run the checker instead of guessing; resolve rule deadlocks deliberately.

entities.label: Tests that constrain behavior
entities.summary: Does the test actually pin behavior?

entities.label: Keep failures visible
entities.summary: Can any error hide?

entities.label: Properties
entities.summary: Generate inputs and assert invariants instead of listing example cases.

entities.label: Mutation grading
entities.summary: Inject bugs and check the tests catch them.

entities.label: Keeping properties honest
entities.summary: Keep properties honest: sourced from design, not the code.

entities.label: Recover system truth
entities.summary: Read the code (names lie), register the footguns, census the silent failures, assemble the atlas.

entities.label: Lock and prove behavior
entities.summary: Characterize, reproduce, prove-no-drift.

entities.label: Working procedures
entities.summary: How to run the work: sizing, de-risking, root-causing, reporting.

entities.label: Tools authoritative
entities.summary: Run the checker; never estimate compliance by reading code.

entities.label: Deadlock protocol
entities.summary: If fixing A re-breaks B, suppress the tool heuristic (not a human constraint), logged.

entities.label: Boundary
entities.summary: For every x op limit, test x == limit.

entities.label: No-op branch
entities.summary: Test the not-triggered branch of conditional mutations.

entities.label: Lifecycle & composition
entities.summary: Post-close behavior, no ignored params, shared-resource ownership.

entities.label: Value-pinning
entities.summary: Assert enum/string outputs by value; every member reachable.

entities.label: Invariant-exercisability
entities.summary: Claim an invariant only if the test exercises it.

entities.label: Contract adequacy
entities.summary: Verdict-over-structured-input needs a property + satisfiability + oracle, not examples.

entities.label: Negatives & edges
entities.summary: Golden + each declared failure mode + each documented edge; no golden-only.

entities.label: Mocking discipline
entities.summary: Mock only external boundaries; minimal per-case stubs.

entities.label: Requirement traceability
entities.summary: Every stated requirement maps to at least one test via a mechanically checkable id; an untraced requirement is unverifie

entities.label: Handle or propagate
entities.summary: No silent third outcome; an observable trace or it flows to a boundary.

entities.label: No blanket catches
entities.summary: Name the exception; broad catch only at a boundary that surfaces it.

entities.label: No swallowed handled
entities.summary: A caught error's resolution must be observable.

entities.label: No orphan async
entities.summary: Every spawned task has a completion observer.

entities.label: Check subprocess results
entities.summary: Exit code + stderr surfaced on failure.

entities.label: Tests must not mask defects
entities.summary: No pattern that turns a framework/import failure into a test pass.

entities.label: Properties over examples
entities.summary: Where behavior ranges over a structured domain, assert invariants via generators.

entities.label: Two sources
entities.summary: Schema-derived shape properties + design-derived behavior invariants.

entities.label: Mutation grades the suite
entities.summary: A test/property that kills no mutant is too weak — fix the test, not the threshold.

entities.label: Triage survivors
entities.summary: A surviving mutant is a weak test (fix it) or dead code (delete it) — read it before blaming the count.

entities.label: Provenance
entities.summary: Properties cite a design source; never authored from the impl (it just restates the code).

entities.label: Adversarial refutation
entities.summary: A 2nd agent writes a wrong impl that still passes — a survivor reveals a missing property.

entities.label: Halt-and-triage
entities.summary: A counterexample halts; classify bug/wrong-property/spec-gap; never auto-relax.

entities.label: Characterize before change
entities.summary: Lock current behavior first; the recovered tests are the spec.

entities.label: Reproducibility by type
entities.summary: Replay if deterministic; else golden-master/snapshot/seam-injection.

entities.label: Adequacy on characterization
entities.summary: Adequacy gates apply to characterization tests too.

entities.label: Footgun register
entities.summary: Record every name-vs-behavior contradiction with a file:line anchor and a date.

entities.label: Silent-failure census
entities.summary: Sweep every site an error can be lost; classify each real-risk vs benign by reading it.

entities.label: System Truth Atlas
entities.summary: Assemble the dated, anchored MAP deliverable: code map + footgun register + risk register + census.

entities.label: Read the system
entities.summary: Names lie → recover ground truth → footgun atlas.

entities.label: Attention-sizing
entities.summary: ~1 min plan + ~1 min review; carve on concerns, not lines/files.

entities.label: De-risk gate
entities.summary: Walking-skeleton the riskiest assumption before committing the plan.

entities.label: PR-sizing
entities.summary: Size to the operator's milestone; two no-engagement lgtm's = wrong unit.

entities.label: Root-cause over symptom
entities.summary: ≥3 divergent symptoms ⇒ audit upstream, name one cause, land a catcher.

entities.label: Cheap checks gate expensive
entities.summary: Run the full $0 deterministic suite before any costly/irreversible step.

entities.label: Report decisions
entities.summary: Surface judgment calls, tradeoffs, suppressions — not a recap of actions.

entities.label: Decision gate
entities.summary: Gate a critical decision on a falsifiable disproof condition, a detector, and proof proportionate to how much could brea

entities.label: Design-assumptions register
entities.summary: Surface the critical assumptions a design rests on; each gets a 'breaks if' falsifier, a consequence-anchored fragility,

entities.label: One meaning per term
entities.summary: A critical spec term carries one meaning; a term with two clear meanings (ambiguity, not vagueness) splits readers silen

entities.label: Cover the mirror
entities.summary: Every operation with a natural inverse implies its inverse; a present half whose mirror is silently missing (create with

entities.label: Design completeness
entities.summary: Before code, each behaviour names its contract, its failure modes, and its edges; a present-but-empty (TBD) section coun

entities.label: System Truth Atlas
entities.summary: Code map + footgun register + risk register + silent-failure census.

entities.label: Silent-failure census
entities.summary: Every place the system can fail without telling you.

entities.label: Characterization suite
entities.summary: Tests that lock current behavior.

entities.label: Reproducibility baseline
entities.summary: The behavior-lock baseline (replay/golden-master).

entities.label: Change plan
entities.summary: Attention-sized PR plan.

entities.label: Walking skeleton
entities.summary: Riskiest-assumption spike.

entities.label: Gated PRs
entities.summary: Changes shipped under mechanical gates.

entities.label: Behavior-preservation proof
entities.summary: Before/after evidence behavior didn't drift.

entities.label: Remediation burn-down
entities.summary: Debt → 0 under gates.

entities.label: Go-forward Constitution
entities.summary: Rules the project commits to and enforces.

entities.label: Operator
entities.summary: Human owner; owns sign-off gates.

entities.label: Builder
entities.summary: Strong-model agent; does the work.

entities.label: Auditor
entities.summary: Agent + CI; verifies independently.

entities.label: Adequacy gap
entities.summary: Tests pass but don't pin behavior.

entities.label: Visibility gap
entities.summary: Code runs but errors hid.

entities.label: Behavior drift
entities.summary: A change silently altered behavior.

entities.label: Property that restates the code
entities.summary: A property mirrors buggy code — mutation can't see it.

entities.label: Architectural risk
entities.summary: A critical assumption proves wrong after commitment.

entities.label: Untrustworthy ground truth
entities.summary: Names, types, and docs contradict what the code does — every claim is a guess.

entities.label: Phase-2 refactor (obsolete-and-replace)
entities.summary: When a mechanism is the defect, obsolete and replace it under the same proof discipline: disposition, a computed blast r

entities.label: Disposition at the gate
entities.summary: The operator assigns each unit a disposition: repair, retire, accept, or consumed-by-a-refactor. Replace is a pointer to

entities.label: Refactor is first-class
entities.summary: A refactor consumes a set of units and produces one mechanism; cardinality unifies consolidation (N), plain replace (1),

entities.label: Consumes / produces / affects
entities.summary: Three edges: the consumed units (retired at cutover), the produced mechanism, and the affected units whose behavior is p

entities.label: Decision-total contract
entities.summary: Keep/drop over consumed behavior plus a preserve-set over affected behavior; every behavior assigned and every call site

entities.label: Blast radius drives cutover
entities.summary: Blast radius is computed from the dependency graph (a lower bound, since runtime resolution hides edges) and chooses ato

entities.label: Pin is a precondition
entities.summary: Pinning precedes a refactor and is demand-driven by its footprint; affected units the blast radius reaches become pin de

entities.label: Surface-scoped pin
entities.summary: Pin state is per-interface, sound only where the surface is a true cut, else widen. The same typed interface is the stru

entities.label: Flag runtime resolution, not DI
entities.summary: Interface-based dependency injection keeps the blast radius static; service locators, reflection, string registries, and

entities.label: Extract the seam first
entities.summary: Where no boundary exists, a coarse baseline then a behavior-preserving extraction (verified a true cut) precedes the rep

entities.label: One typed graph; views are projections
entities.summary: Blast radius and the precondition DAG are filtered projections over one typed graph; the roadmap list stays primary, the

entities.label: Seam placement is a decision-gate
entities.summary: Placement runs on a provisional seam with a walking-skeleton proof; the kill-condition is the downstream contract failin

entities.label: Refactor draft
entities.summary: A non-destructive, replayable ledger overlay: the consumed set, the produced mechanism, the contract, and a gated status

entities.label: Keep/drop contract
entities.summary: The decision-total partition of consumed behavior plus the preserve-set over affected behavior.

entities.label: Blast radius
entities.summary: The computed set of dependents of a seam, with opaque runtime-resolution sites flagged as a lower-bound caveat.

entities.label: Extracted seam
entities.summary: An interface introduced where none existed, verified a true cut, with its characterization (a reusable asset).

entities.label: Rewrite-and-pray
entities.summary: Obsoleting a mechanism and shipping the replacement without preserving the behavior that was correct: the escape hatch f

entities.label: reference.md#pdd-map
entities.oneLine: The full MAP command: cost-ordered sweep → atlas → classify → carve, subsystem ranking formula, providers, atlas cache, and the validated write boundary.

entities.label: reference.md#pdd-roadmap--the-curate-gate
entities.oneLine: The curate-gate CLI: append-only ledger replayed over the pristine carve, approve/deny/edit/move/split/refactor/undo, and sign-off as the Phase-1 → Phase-2 handoff.

entities.label: reference.md#unit-anatomy
entities.oneLine: What one carved unit carries: finding fingerprints, risk/size with explanatory basis, the riskScore formula, and byte-identical determinism across hosts.

entities.label: reference.md#pdd-remediate
entities.oneLine: The per-unit fix loop: detached worktree, characterization guard, scope check, re-sweep gate, and the one-branch one-commit-per-unit provenance contract.

entities.label: reference.md#pdd-check-and-the-gates
entities.oneLine: The nine mechanical gates, their default block/warn modes per flow, exit-code semantics, and the boundary-tests-is-a-proxy caveat.

entities.label: reference.md#config-pddconfigyaml
entities.oneLine: The v2 flows-list config: one flow per language with its own paths, gates, testCommand and mutation settings, plus the single-flow execution caveat.

entities.label: reference.md#pdd-init
entities.oneLine: What init scaffolds idempotently: config.yaml per detected language, constitution.md as committed law, and a pre-commit hook running pdd check --staged.

entities.label: reference.md#pdd-prove-vs-pdd-grade
entities.oneLine: The two mutation commands kept distinct: prove is the targeted boundary oracle that can fail the build, grade is broad diagnostic mutation with nine operators and a per-unit cache.

entities.label: reference.md#the-gate-budget
entities.oneLine: The third sizing axis (mutation cost): the estimate formula, the durable calibration ledger, the budget-aware carve split, and warn-first enforcement.

entities.label: reference.md#dashboards
entities.oneLine: The three loopback-only servers (dashboard 4317, plan 4318, gallery 4319) and the shared posture that viewers never write .pdd/ except by spawning the CLI.

entities.label: reference.md#the-pdd-directory
entities.oneLine: Which .pdd/ artifacts are committed vs runtime, the sole-writer-per-artifact table, and the atomic-write guarantee against torn state.

entities.label: reference.md#agent-seams-how-a-session-drives-pdd
entities.oneLine: The Model B contract — the session authors, the CLI validates and writes — via worklist → ingest/classify, with the same posture on remediation and dashboards.

entities.label: core.md#recover-and-lock-behavior
entities.oneLine: The MAP/PIN rules: read the system not the names, record footguns, census silent failures, assemble the atlas, then characterize and make behavior reproducible before change.

entities.label: core.md#tests-that-constrain-behavior
entities.oneLine: The adequacy rules a test must meet to constrain anything: boundary, no-op path, value pinning, whole-domain properties, edge coverage, requirement tracing, mock-only-edges.

entities.label: core.md#keep-failures-visible
entities.oneLine: The no-silent-failure rules: handle-or-propagate with a visible trace, named catches, no fire-and-forget, checked subprocess exits, tests that can't hide a broken build.

entities.label: core.md#grading-the-tests-themselves
entities.oneLine: The layer that checks the suite as a whole bites: properties from schema and design, mutation grading with the weak-test-vs-dead-code read, beat-the-properties, stop on counterexamples.

entities.label: core.md#working-procedures
entities.oneLine: How to run the work: size to attention and to the gate budget, derisk and decision-gate before committing, register assumptions, one meaning per term, size PRs to the operator, report decisions.

entities.label: core.md#refactor-obsolete-and-replace
entities.oneLine: The rules that keep replace-not-repair honest: disposition at the gate, one refactor object with three edges, decision-total keep/drop, computed blast radius, pin before replace.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: assert-by-shape — a test returning a structured value compares the whole fixture in one deep-equality assertion, not a column of per-field pokes.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: boundary-tests — any comparison against a limit must have a test case at the boundary itself, not only values to either side.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: characterize-before-change — pin untrusted code's current behavior in adequacy-grade tests before touching it, so the tests become the recovered spec.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: cohesion-review — when a change grows an existing module, review the whole resulting module for semantic duplication and responsibility drift, not just the diff.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: cover-the-mirror — every operation with a natural inverse (create/delete, acquire/release) must have its other half present or explicitly marked absent-by-design.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: decision-gate — gate a hard-to-reverse decision with an observable kill-condition, a detector, and proof sized to blast radius, ending in GREEN / RED / OVERRIDE.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: decompose-by-attention — carve work into attention-sized pieces of roughly one minute of operator planning plus one minute of review.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: derisk-gate — before committing a plan, prove the riskiest architectural assumptions with a thin end-to-end walking skeleton and a ranked risk register.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: design-assumptions-register — surface the never-consciously-chosen assumptions a plan rests on and record each with a falsifying condition, fragility, and detector.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: design-completeness — a design must name each behavior's contract, failure modes, and edges before code; a TBD section is a gap wearing the costume of completeness.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: footgun-register — when a name, type, or signature contradicts real runtime behavior, record appearance, verified reality, file:line anchor, and date in a committed register.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: keep-properties-honest — guard against tautological properties: source each from the design never the code, try to beat the suite with a wrong implementation, classify every counterexample.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: mutation-grading — grade whether tests actually bite by injecting small bugs; every surviving mutant is a weak test to fix or dead code to delete, so read each one.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: no-op-paths — any conditionally-mutating method (prune, evict, expire, gc) needs a test where the trigger is not met and post-state equals pre-state.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: one-meaning-per-term — a load-bearing spec term must carry exactly one meaning; a word that passes the two-meanings test is a defect to split or disambiguate.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: pr-sizing — bundle or ship changes by the operator's attention (one reviewable milestone per PR), not by the agent's construction unit.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: properties — pin behavior over structured input with generated properties from two sources, the data's schema and the design, instead of a handful of examples.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: read-the-system — before any runtime-behavior claim about unfamiliar code, open and read the implementation; a name or grep hit is a lead, not a fact.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: reproducibility-baseline — build a no-drift baseline before changing behavior-critical code: replay-and-diff if deterministic, else golden-master or injected clock/RNG/I-O seams.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: silent-failure-census — sweep for every place that can fail without a trace (swallowed exceptions, ignored returns, fire-and-forget) and classify each real-vs-benign.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: system-truth-atlas — assemble MAP's deliverable: a committed diagnostic of verified code map, footgun register, recovered-risk register, and silent-failure census.

entities.label: SKILL.md
entities.oneLine: trace-requirements-to-tests — every stated requirement carries a mechanically checkable link to a test that would fail if it were violated; no link, no coverage claim.

entities.label: budget-aware-chunking.md
entities.oneLine: Implementation plan for the machine-cost sizing axis: estimate each chunk's mutation cost from a calibrated model and split folder groups in CARVE until they fit the gate budget.

entities.label: five-model-experiment.md
entities.oneLine: Five-arm experiment (raw vs PDD across Opus/Sonnet/Haiku on cli/src): the PDD taxonomy raises precision at every capable tier while trading high-severity recall.

entities.label: map-report-format.md
entities.oneLine: Design spec for the buyer-credible MAP diagnostic report: the translation layer that turns findings/units/mutation output into a document a CTO reads and acts on.

entities.label: multi-language-flows.md
entities.oneLine: Design for polyglot repos: a project stays one .pdd/ but declares a set of language-flows, each a complete single-language pipeline with its own gates and test runner.

entities.label: phase2-refactor-model.md
entities.oneLine: Design decisions for obsolete-and-replace: disposition chosen per unit at the curate gate, the refactor as a shared object with consume/produce/affects edges and computed blast radius.

entities.label: python-pack.md
entities.oneLine: The Python gate-pack plan (PRs 1-6) behind the stdio pack contract: libcst-based byte-splice mutants, with PR1's derisk skeleton already GREEN on a real pytest loop.

entities.label: selfrun-pdd-vs-control.md
entities.oneLine: Self-run experiment on PDD's own CLI: PDD vs an unstructured agent was a near-tie on raw finds but PDD won precision (97% vs 74%) and produced a proven test-adequacy grade the control cannot.

entities.label: pbt-pilot-compiler.md
entities.oneLine: Field report on a real TypeScript game compiler, run under an enforce-or-drop rule: the property-plus-mutation layer bites, but narrowly and predictably, and where it bites was the more useful finding.

entities.label: roadmap dossier
entities.payload: GET /api/roadmap (plan server :4318, and gallery :4319 as /api/roadmap?project=<name>). Shape = RoadmapPayload (cli/src/commands/plan.ts:196): verdict + counts (approved/denied/pending), budgetMinutes, and units[] where each unit carries id, subsystem, rank, risk/size + riskBasis/sizeBasis (the 'why'), findingFingerprints, joined findings[] (ProjectedFinding: title, file:line locus, clickable href via SourceLink GitHub-blob or editor link, classification badge, evidence key/value pairs, siteCount) plus summary (atlas code-map context line) and cost (CostComparison est→actual (drift), overBudget flag), plus refactors[] with computed blast radius. Built by roadmapPayload → joinRoadmapFindings (pure, unit-tested).
entities.renderer: Gallery TS component cli/ui/src/components/RoadmapView.tsx rendered with interactive={false} (verdict strip, unit rows with risk dot / rank / risk-size / findings count / CostChip, expandable per-finding dossier with evidence and lock-badged private links). Same payload also drives the plan-dashboard's interactive roadmap tab (cli/plan-dashboard/app.js renderRoadmap, unit rows + refactor meta-rows) — for a public doc the read-only RoadmapView is the right renderer.
entities.snapshotHow: Gallery-export precedent applies DIRECTLY — the public alextavor.github.io/gallery was built by rebuilding the esbuild bundle (npm run build:ui) with fetch('/api/roadmap?project=X') rewritten to static /gallery/api/*.json, curled from a live `pdd gallery /Users/alextavor/Documents/Alex/pdd-corpus`. For a narrative-doc modal: curl http://127.0.0.1:4319/api/roadmap?project=<name> > api/roadmap-<name>-2026-07-03.json and point the rewritten RoadmapView bundle (or an inlined copy) at it. Payload is fully self-contained (findings already joined server-side), so the static snapshot renders identically.
entities.chapter: CARVE — the risk-ranked remediation roadmap, and why each unit ranks where it does

entities.label: corpus rail
entities.payload: GET /api/corpus (gallery :4319). Shape = CorpusListing (cli/src/corpus.ts:27): root + projects[] {name, pddDir (ABSOLUTE local path — strip or ignore for public), domain, verdict (static-gate pass/fail), units (live unitCount from the snapshot's units.json, manifest fallback), grade ('68%'), ranFullMap, visibility}. Manifest-first from <corpus>/index.json (live corpus at /Users/alextavor/Documents/Alex/pdd-corpus), derived directory-scan fallback.
entities.renderer: Gallery Rail component in cli/ui/src/gallery/main.tsx (project buttons with domain line + verdict/units/grade chips, 'static only' warn chip when ranFullMap is false).
entities.snapshotHow: Already snapshotted once by the gallery-export precedent (the public gallery's /gallery/api/corpus.json IS this payload, curled from a live gallery). Dated modal snapshot: curl http://127.0.0.1:4319/api/corpus > api/corpus-2026-07-03.json; scrub the pddDir absolute paths (the rail never displays them; only the live server uses them for name→dir resolution).
entities.chapter: The corpus — what has been analyzed so far, at a glance

entities.label: project summary
entities.payload: GET /api/summary?project=<name> (gallery :4319). Shape = ProjectSummary (cli/src/corpus.ts:152): the raw manifest entry passed through (loc, sourceFiles/testFiles/testCount, languages/tsPercent, duplication, git age/active span, coverage, stars, license, lastPush, analyzedAt, analysis.gradeScore + gradeMutants killed/total, fullMapUnits, checkVerdict), findings totals computed live from the .pdd (total, bySeverity, byKind — grade survivors collapsed to 'test-gap' —, byStatus/classification), and run (model / costUsd or tokens / durationMs) or null ('not recorded').
entities.renderer: cli/ui/src/components/SummaryPanel.tsx (title + domain·framework·architecture sub, provenance line, RunRow, 10-stat grid, severity/kind/status chip rows, and the fixed trust paragraph 'Every issue here was schema-validated…').
entities.snapshotHow: Gallery-export precedent applies (the public gallery snapshots this per project as static JSON). curl http://127.0.0.1:4319/api/summary?project=<name> > api/summary-<name>-2026-07-03.json; renders offline with SummaryPanel unchanged since everything is precomputed server-side.
entities.chapter: One project's diagnostic card — cost, scale, adequacy, findings totals

entities.label: build plan gantt
entities.payload: GET /api/plan (plan server :4318). Shape = planPayload (cli/src/commands/plan.ts:78): the durable .pdd/plan.json items (id, title, phase, status, deps, …) + server-derived graph (DAG depth/order + warnings: cycles, dangling deps) + the live progress overlay (.pdd/.run/plan-progress.json: activeItemId, lastEvent, per-item statuses that win over static ones) + repo name. Companion GET /api/plan/health returns just the warnings.
entities.renderer: Plan-dashboard vanilla app.js 'build plan' view (cli/plan-dashboard/app.js): renderGantt + renderPhaseChips + renderDetail (per-item dependency panel, 'depends on … none — startable now'), renderNow ('now building X' dot), renderHealth (plan-graph warnings banner). No TS-gallery equivalent yet (memory: app.js is the legacy surface, new UI routes to the gallery).
entities.snapshotHow: No export precedent for the plan-dashboard — two options: (a) same fetch-rewrite trick applied to the vanilla app.js (point getJson('/api/plan') at a dated static JSON; app.js has only 3 GET fetches on this view, and the POST seams are dead in a static context); (b) simpler for a narrative modal: curl http://127.0.0.1:4318/api/plan > api/plan-2026-07-03.json and screenshot the live gantt, since the interactive view (click-through detail, live poll) adds little to a frozen story beat. The underlying .pdd/plan.json is durable and committed, so a git-SHA-pinned copy is also a valid dated snapshot (the graph derivation is deterministic).
entities.chapter: PDD building itself — the self-hosted plan as gantt

entities.label: map run tracker
entities.payload: GET /api/map-run (plan server :4318). Shape = MapRunPayload (cli/src/commands/plan.ts:274): run {phase, state running/done/aborted, sweep [{name, code}], provider, skipSweep, startedAt, finishedAt} from .pdd/.run/map.json, atlas {state, global done/total subsystems + authored counts} and classify {state, global total/done/kept/dropped} rollups, carve.units (the EFFECTIVE ledger-replayed unit count). null run = no MAP driven yet.
entities.renderer: Plan-dashboard app.js renderMapRun (cli/plan-dashboard/app.js:812): the 4-step tracker sweep → atlas → classify → carve with per-step detail strings ('12/12 subsystems', '40 kept · 3 dropped', 'N units') and running/aborted/done header states; renderLaunch is the paired write control (irrelevant static).
entities.snapshotHow: TRANSIENT — lives in .pdd/.run/, wiped between runs, so the snapshot must be curled at the moment of interest: capture once mid-run (state 'running', a step active) and once at completion ('MAP complete — roadmap ready below') during a real MAP; save as api/map-run-<project>-2026-07-03.json. No gallery precedent (gallery has no run endpoints); a static modal would replay the two frozen states, which is exactly the narrative beat ('watch the MAP resolve').
entities.chapter: MAP running — the pipeline resolving into a roadmap

entities.label: remediation worklist
entities.payload: GET /api/remediation (plan server :4318). Shape = RemediationRoadmap (cli/src/remediation/status.ts:88): verdict GREEN/PENDING/EMPTY, finalized (operator signed off), units[] = the APPROVED roadmap units in carve order, each {unit: WorkingUnit, status: {state pending|in-progress|green|needs-hitl|committed|error, attempts, startedAt, updatedAt, note}} joined from durable .pdd/remediation/status.json.
entities.renderer: NONE yet — no dashboard or gallery component fetches /api/remediation (verified: only tests and CLI help reference it; `pdd remediate` prints 'watch live on `pdd plan` (/api/remediation)' but the UI view is unbuilt). A narrative modal would need a purpose-built table (state-colored rows mirroring the roadmap rows) or show curated raw JSON.
entities.snapshotHow: curl http://127.0.0.1:4318/api/remediation > api/remediation-2026-07-03.json (durable inputs — units.json + ledger + remediation/status.json — so it re-mints identically from a committed .pdd snapshot). Gallery-export precedent does NOT directly apply (no existing renderer to rebundle); cheapest honest snapshot is the JSON itself plus the state-lifecycle legend from status.ts.
entities.chapter: REMEDIATE — the approved units moving through the fix loop

entities.label: merged findings
entities.payload: GET /api/findings (findings dashboard :4317). Shape = mergeFindings (cli/src/mutation/findings.ts): slices[] provenance (source check/prove/map/grade:<id>, generatedAt, verdict/score), gates[] (gate, mode block/warn, verdict, finding count, summary), findings[] + rolled[] (deduped: fingerprint, title, detail, severity, kind footgun/risk/silent-failure/code-map/mutant-operator, locus file:line, classification overlay from .pdd/findings/classifications.json), counts facets (bySource/bySeverity/byStatus/byClassification), repo name.
entities.renderer: Findings dashboard cli/dashboard/app.js: renderGates (the gate/mode/verdict strip that subsumes `pdd status`), renderFacets (AND-combined filter chips), findings list + per-finding detail pane. No TS-gallery equivalent (the gallery consumes the same rolled findings only via the roadmap join).
entities.snapshotHow: No export precedent; curl http://127.0.0.1:4317/api/findings > api/findings-2026-07-03.json. Inputs are all durable (.pdd/findings/*.json + units/*/mutation.json), so it also re-mints from a committed snapshot. For the doc, the roadmap dossier usually tells this story better (findings pre-joined to units); use this payload when the beat is the raw gate strip / facet counts.
entities.chapter: The gate verdicts and the raw finding stream, before carving

entities.label: grade status grid
entities.payload: GET /api/status (findings dashboard :4317). Shape = .pdd/.run/grade-status.json: engine, startedAt, global {unitsTotal, unitsDone, running, queued, stale, errored}, units[] {id, scope, state, score, survivors, mutantsRun/Total, durationMs, loc, hash}. Companions /api/atlas-run and /api/classify-run stream the per-item atlas/classify progress the same way.
entities.renderer: Findings dashboard app.js async-jobs tracker + score-colored unit grid (cli/dashboard/app.js, poll at line 377; atlas/classify row renderers around line 180).
entities.snapshotHow: Transient (.run/) — curl during or right after a live grade: curl http://127.0.0.1:4317/api/status > api/grade-status-2026-07-03.json. Lower narrative value than mutation.json itself (below), which is durable and carries the actual survivors; use this only for a 'grade in flight' beat.
entities.chapter: PROVE in flight — the mutation grade streaming

entities.label: unit mutation survivors
entities.payload: GET /api/unit/<id> (findings dashboard :4317) = the durable file .pdd/units/<id>/mutation.json verbatim. Shape = GradeResults (cli/src/commands/grade-results.ts:32): scope, engine, gradedAt, durationMs, loc, totals {mutants, killed, survived, uncovered, stale}, score, and survivors[] {file, line, operator, snippet, status survived|uncovered, ranTests, classification 'pending'} — the fault-injection receipts.
entities.renderer: Findings dashboard app.js survivor-triage detail (per-unit drill-down from the grid); survivors also surface as grade findings in /api/findings and as the cost 'actual' on roadmap units.
entities.snapshotHow: Easiest of all: the artifact is ALREADY a durable dated file on disk — copy .pdd/units/<id>/mutation.json (gradedAt is the date stamp) into the doc's api/ dir, no server needed. A survivor list with real snippet + ranTests is the single most persuasive proof artifact for the 'adequacy proven by fault injection, not estimated' claim the SummaryPanel makes.
entities.chapter: Proof — the mutants that survived, with the exact snippet and the tests that ran

entities.label: units json carve baseline
entities.payload: Non-API durable artifact .pdd/units.json. Shape = UnitsDoc (cli/src/units.ts:59): version, generatedAt, depth, order[], units[] {id, title, subsystem, scope globs, size/risk + riskBasis/sizeBasis, findingFingerprints, dependsOn, rank, state, curated, estMinutes, riskScore + riskScoreBasis {score, exposure, findings, fanIn, covGap}}. Deterministic, byte-identical re-carve.
entities.renderer: Never rendered raw — /api/roadmap is its presentable projection (ledger replayed + findings joined + cost attached). For the doc, show a trimmed excerpt (one unit's riskScoreBasis) as a code block beside the rendered roadmap modal to make 'the ranking is traceable' concrete.
entities.snapshotHow: Copy the committed file at a git SHA (it is durable, allowlisted in .pdd/, and generatedAt-stamped) — no server, no rewrite. If a rendered view is wanted, mint it via the roadmap-dossier candidate instead.
entities.chapter: CARVE internals — the deterministic baseline the roadmap derives from

entities.label: roadmap ledger audit
entities.payload: Non-API durable artifact .pdd/roadmap-ledger.json (cli/src/roadmap.ts:47). Append-only, replayable audit of HITL decisions: entries[] of approve | deny | edit | move | split | signoff ops with timestamps; the effective roadmap is derived by replaying it over units.json, so every decision (even overridden ones) survives as a reviewable git diff.
entities.renderer: No direct renderer — its EFFECT renders as decision badges/counts in /api/roadmap (RoadmapView verdict strip: 'N approved · N denied · N pending') and as strike-through curation in the plan-dashboard roadmap tab. For the doc: a short entries excerpt as a code block ('the audit trail'), paired with before/after roadmap snapshots.
entities.snapshotHow: Copy the committed file (append-only, entries self-dated). The compelling static story is a pair: roadmap payload curled before a curation session and after, plus the ledger diff between them — all three re-mintable from two git SHAs of the .pdd snapshot.
entities.chapter: HITL curation — decisions as an append-only, replayable ledger

entities.label: calibration ledger
entities.payload: Non-API durable artifact .pdd/calibration.jsonl (cli/src/calibration.ts): append-only JSONL of real proof-run costs, one CalibrationRecord per line (per-run wallMs/mutants/loc datapoints; seeded with the compiler datapoint), consumed by cost.ts fit — the source of the secPerMutant constant (2.38 measured vs 0.30 assumed) and the est/actual/drift chips on roadmap units.
entities.renderer: No UI renderer — it surfaces indirectly as CostChip drift on RoadmapView / app.js costChip and the budget line 'budget Nm'. For the doc: render the records as a small table or scatter (est vs actual) in the modal; the drift-correction story ('we assumed 0.30s/mutant, the ledger said 2.38') is the beat.
entities.snapshotHow: Copy the file (records are self-dated, append order meaningless by contract). No precedent needed — it is already static.
entities.chapter: Budget-aware chunking — calibrating the cost model on real runs

entities.label: constitution
entities.payload: Non-API durable artifact .pdd/constitution.md — the human-readable law: active gates each with mode (block/warn) and a one-line rationale ('the off-by-one lives at x == limit', 'a failure that leaves no trace looks healthy while it's wrong'), plus the modes legend. .pdd/config.yaml is its machine-readable twin.
entities.renderer: No UI renderer (it is prose by design). In the doc, render the markdown directly in a modal — it already reads as narrative and pairs with the gate strip from /api/findings (the same gates, live verdicts).
entities.snapshotHow: Copy the committed markdown at a git SHA; add the SHA/date in the modal caption. Zero minting work.
entities.chapter: The constitution — what this project commits to, and what blocks a merge
